mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, invalidating the short-term bearish signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles yield negative 20-day forward returns in > 60% of observed cases, breaking the regime-specific offset mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, elevated discount rates compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, negating the valuation compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 20-day horizons with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect sustained aggregate demand growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals severe supply-side contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances following an INDPRO drop greater than 1.0%, invalidating the contraction signal.
